features.description: Next.js project scaffolded with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui initialized
steps: Verify package.json exists with next, react, typescript, tailwindcss dependencies
Run 'npx tsc --noEmit' and confirm zero errors
Navigate to http://localhost:3000 and verify the page loads
Verify tailwind.config.ts exists and is configured
Verify tsconfig.json has strict mode enabled

features.description: Prisma schema defined with all data models (RegulatoryItem, PolicyDocument, PolicyChunk, Alert, AuditEntry, IngestionRun, LlmCallLog) and SQLite database connected
steps: Verify prisma/schema.prisma exists with all 7 models
Run 'npx prisma generate' successfully
Run 'npx prisma db push' successfully
Verify src/lib/db.ts exports a singleton Prisma client
Import the Prisma client in a test file and confirm it connects

features.description: Environment configuration with .env.example containing all required variables and .env.local loading correctly
steps: Verify .env.example exists with DATABASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, EMBEDDING_PROVIDER, OPENAI_API_KEY, VOYAGE_API_KEY, EMBEDDING_MODEL, CLAUDE_MODEL, INGESTION_INTERVAL_HOURS, LOG_LEVEL
Verify .env.local is created from .env.example by init.sh
Verify Next.js loads environment variables at runtime

features.description: Shared TypeScript types defined in src/types/index.ts covering all domain types used across the application
steps: Verify src/types/index.ts exists
Verify it exports types for RegulatoryItem, PolicyDocument, PolicyChunk, Alert, AuditEntry, IngestionRun, DriftClassification, Severity, AlertStatus, Regulator
Run 'npx tsc --noEmit' and confirm zero errors

features.description: Application shell with fixed left sidebar navigation (Dashboard, Alerts, Ingestion Log) and top header bar showing 'Tether - Regulatory Drift Detector'
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000
Verify left sidebar is visible with 240px width
Verify sidebar contains navigation links: Dashboard, Alerts, Ingestion Log
Verify header bar displays 'Tether' as app title and 'Regulatory Drift Detector' as subtitle
Click each sidebar link and verify it navigates to the correct page

features.description: Active navigation state highlights the current page in the sidebar
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000 (Dashboard)
Verify Dashboard link is visually highlighted as active
Navigate to /alerts
Verify Alerts link is now highlighted and Dashboard is not
Navigate to /ingestion
Verify Ingestion Log link is now highlighted

features.description: Health check endpoint returns status, timestamp, and database connection status
steps: Send GET request to /api/health
Verify response contains { status: 'ok', timestamp: <ISO string>, db: 'connected' }
Verify response status code is 200

features.description: BSA/AML dummy policy document created with realistic sections covering anti-money laundering program requirements
steps: Verify data/policies/bsa-aml.md exists
Verify it contains Purpose, Scope, Definitions, and domain-specific sections
Verify it is marked as synthetic in the header
Verify it is 2-5 pages of substantive policy language

features.description: Consumer complaint handling dummy policy document created
steps: Verify data/policies/complaint-handling.md exists
Verify it contains Purpose, Scope, Definitions, and domain-specific sections
Verify it is marked as synthetic and is 2-5 pages

features.description: Fair lending dummy policy document created
steps: Verify data/policies/fair-lending.md exists
Verify it contains Purpose, Scope, Definitions, and domain-specific sections
Verify it is marked as synthetic and is 2-5 pages

features.description: Regulation E (electronic fund transfers) dummy policy document created
steps: Verify data/policies/reg-e.md exists
Verify it contains Purpose, Scope, Definitions, and domain-specific sections
Verify it is marked as synthetic and is 2-5 pages

features.description: Regulation Z (truth in lending) dummy policy document created
steps: Verify data/policies/reg-z.md exists
Verify it contains Purpose, Scope, Definitions, and domain-specific sections
Verify it is marked as synthetic and is 2-5 pages

features.description: Vendor management dummy policy document created
steps: Verify data/policies/vendor-management.md exists
Verify it contains Purpose, Scope, Definitions, and domain-specific sections
Verify it is marked as synthetic and is 2-5 pages

features.description: Information security and incident response dummy policy document created
steps: Verify data/policies/info-security.md exists
Verify it contains Purpose, Scope, Definitions, and domain-specific sections
Verify it is marked as synthetic and is 2-5 pages

features.description: Customer identification program (CIP) dummy policy document created
steps: Verify data/policies/cip.md exists
Verify it contains Purpose, Scope, Definitions, and domain-specific sections
Verify it is marked as synthetic and is 2-5 pages

features.description: Overdraft practices dummy policy document created
steps: Verify data/policies/overdraft.md exists
Verify it contains Purpose, Scope, Definitions, and domain-specific sections
Verify it is marked as synthetic and is 2-5 pages

features.description: Marketing and advertising review dummy policy document created
steps: Verify data/policies/marketing.md exists
Verify it contains Purpose, Scope, Definitions, and domain-specific sections
Verify it is marked as synthetic and is 2-5 pages

features.description: Prisma seed script loads all 10 policy documents into PolicyDocument table and chunks them into PolicyChunk records split at ## headings
steps: Run 'npx prisma db seed'
Query PolicyDocument table and verify 10 records exist
Query PolicyChunk table and verify each document has multiple chunks
Verify each chunk has sectionHeading and content populated
Verify chunkIndex is sequential within each document

features.description: Embedding generation utility in src/lib/ai/embeddings.ts that generates vector embeddings for text input using the configured provider
steps: Verify src/lib/ai/embeddings.ts exists and exports a generateEmbedding function
Call generateEmbedding with a sample text string
Verify it returns a numeric array (float vector)
Verify the embedding provider is read from environment variables

features.description: Policy chunk embeddings generated and stored during seed so that all PolicyChunk records have a non-null embedding field
steps: Run the seed script
Query PolicyChunk table
Verify every chunk has a non-null embedding field
Verify embeddings are valid JSON arrays of numbers

features.description: Vector similarity utility in src/lib/vectors.ts computes cosine similarity between two embedding vectors
steps: Verify src/lib/vectors.ts exists and exports cosineSimilarity function
Call cosineSimilarity with two identical vectors and verify result is ~1.0
Call cosineSimilarity with two orthogonal vectors and verify result is ~0.0
Call cosineSimilarity with opposite vectors and verify result is ~-1.0

features.description: Semantic retrieval function in src/lib/drift/retriever.ts returns the top 15 most similar policy chunks for a given text input, with minimum similarity threshold of 0.3
steps: Verify src/lib/drift/retriever.ts exists and exports retrieveCandidates function
Call retrieveCandidates with a sample regulatory text
Verify it returns at most 15 PolicyChunk records
Verify all returned chunks have similarity >= 0.3
Verify results are sorted by similarity descending

features.description: LLM wrapper function logs every Claude API call to the LlmCallLog table with prompt, response, model, tokens, and latency
steps: Make a test LLM call through the wrapper
Query LlmCallLog table and verify a new record was created
Verify the record contains purpose, model, prompt, response, tokenInput, tokenOutput, latencyMs
Verify latencyMs is a positive number

features.description: LLM prompt templates defined in src/lib/ai/prompts.ts for relevance classification, drift classification, and explanation generation
steps: Verify src/lib/ai/prompts.ts exists
Verify it exports prompt templates for: relevanceClassification, driftClassification
Verify each template accepts the required input variables
Verify templates instruct the model to produce structured JSON output

features.description: Relevance classifier determines whether a regulatory item is relevant to any policy domain and returns relevant domains
steps: Call the relevance classifier with a regulatory item about AML rules
Verify it returns { isRelevant: true, relevantDomains: ['bsa_aml'], reasoning: <string> }
Call with an item clearly unrelated to banking (e.g., agriculture regulation)
Verify it returns { isRelevant: false, relevantDomains: [], reasoning: <string> }
Verify structured output conforms to the expected JSON schema

features.description: Drift classifier evaluates a regulatory item against a policy chunk and returns classification, confidence, explanation, and exact quotes
steps: Call the drift classifier with a regulatory text and a contradicting policy chunk
Verify it returns one of: aligned, drifted, contradicted, ambiguous, no_material_impact
Verify confidence is a number between 0.0 and 1.0
Verify explanation is 2-4 sentences
Verify regulatoryQuote and policyQuote are non-empty strings

features.description: Citation verification checks that regulatoryQuote appears in the regulatory item text and policyQuote appears in the policy chunk text
steps: Verify src/lib/drift/citation.ts exports a verifyCitations function
Call verifyCitations with quotes that exist in the source texts and verify it returns true
Call verifyCitations with a fabricated quote not in the source and verify it returns false
Verify that when verification fails, the alert is suppressed (not created)

features.description: Severity scorer derives severity from classification and confidence according to the scoring rules in the app spec
steps: Verify src/lib/drift/scorer.ts exports a deriveSeverity function
Call with classification='contradicted', confidence=0.8 and verify severity='high'
Call with classification='contradicted', confidence=0.5 and verify severity='medium'
Call with classification='drifted', confidence=0.9 and verify severity='medium'
Call with classification='drifted', confidence=0.6 and verify severity='low'
Call with classification='ambiguous', confidence=0.9 and verify severity='low'

features.description: Full drift detection pipeline orchestrator runs all steps (relevance, retrieval, classification, citation verification, scoring, alert creation) for a given regulatory item
steps: Verify src/lib/drift/detector.ts exports runDriftDetection function
Seed the database with policy corpus
Create a test regulatory item that should trigger drift
Call runDriftDetection with the item ID
Verify alerts were created in the Alert table with all required fields populated
Verify AuditEntry records were created for each alert with actor='system' and action='created'

features.description: Drift detection pipeline suppresses alerts when citation verification fails and increments the itemsSuppressed counter on the ingestion run
steps: Create a test scenario where the LLM returns a fabricated quote
Run the pipeline
Verify no alert is created for the fabricated citation
Verify the ingestion run's itemsSuppressed count was incremented

features.description: SEC parser fetches regulatory items from SEC.gov public sources and returns structured RawRegulatoryItem objects
steps: Verify src/lib/ingestion/parsers/sec.ts exists and exports fetchLatest
Call fetchLatest and verify it returns an array of items
Verify each item has sourceUrl, regulator='SEC', publicationDate, documentType, title, fullText
Verify the parser handles network errors gracefully without crashing

features.description: FINRA parser fetches regulatory notices from FINRA.org and returns structured RawRegulatoryItem objects
steps: Verify src/lib/ingestion/parsers/finra.ts exists and exports fetchLatest
Call fetchLatest and verify it returns an array of items
Verify each item has regulator='FINRA' and required fields
Verify the parser handles network errors gracefully

features.description: CFPB parser fetches publications from CFPB.gov and returns structured RawRegulatoryItem objects
steps: Verify src/lib/ingestion/parsers/cfpb.ts exists and exports fetchLatest
Call fetchLatest and verify it returns an array of items
Verify each item has regulator='CFPB' and required fields
Verify the parser handles network errors gracefully

features.description: OCC parser fetches bulletins and issuances from OCC.gov and returns structured RawRegulatoryItem objects
steps: Verify src/lib/ingestion/parsers/occ.ts exists and exports fetchLatest
Call fetchLatest and verify it returns an array of items
Verify each item has regulator='OCC' and required fields
Verify the parser handles network errors gracefully

features.description: HTTP fetcher utility handles timeouts, 404s, and connection errors gracefully by logging the error and returning a failure result instead of throwing
steps: Verify src/lib/ingestion/fetcher.ts exists
Call the fetcher with a known-bad URL and verify it returns an error result (not throws)
Call the fetcher with a valid URL and verify it returns the content
Verify errors are logged with context about which URL failed

features.description: Ingestion pipeline orchestrator creates an IngestionRun record, calls all four parsers, deduplicates by sourceUrl, persists new items, runs drift detection, and updates run metrics
steps: Verify src/lib/ingestion/pipeline.ts exports runIngestion function
Call runIngestion with trigger='manual'
Verify an IngestionRun record is created with status='running' then updated to 'completed'
Verify itemsProcessed count matches the number of new items
Verify duplicate sourceUrls are skipped
Verify the run completes even if one parser fails

features.description: A failed ingestion run does not block subsequent runs from executing
steps: Trigger an ingestion run that fails (e.g., all parsers return errors)
Verify the run is marked as 'failed' with error details
Trigger another ingestion run immediately after
Verify the second run starts and completes independently

features.description: Dashboard stats endpoint returns alert counts by severity, regulator, domain, and status, plus recent ingestion runs
steps: Seed the database with sample alerts across different severities, regulators, and domains
Send GET request to /api/dashboard/stats
Verify response contains alertsBySeverity with high, medium, low counts
Verify response contains alertsByRegulator with SEC, FINRA, CFPB, OCC counts
Verify response contains alertsByDomain with counts per policy domain
Verify response contains totalOpen count matching open alerts
Verify response contains recentIngestion array with up to 5 recent runs

features.description: Alerts list endpoint returns paginated alerts with nested regulatory item and policy chunk summaries
steps: Seed the database with 30+ alerts
Send GET request to /api/alerts
Verify response contains alerts array, total, page, pageSize, totalPages
Verify default pageSize is 25
Verify each alert includes regulatoryItem summary and policyChunk summary
Send GET to /api/alerts?page=2 and verify second page of results

features.description: Alerts list endpoint supports filtering by regulator, severity, status, domain, and date range
steps: Seed alerts with varied regulators, severities, and statuses
Send GET to /api/alerts?regulator=SEC and verify only SEC alerts returned
Send GET to /api/alerts?severity=high and verify only high-severity alerts
Send GET to /api/alerts?status=open and verify only open alerts
Send GET to /api/alerts?domain=bsa_aml and verify only BSA/AML domain alerts
Send GET to /api/alerts?dateFrom=2026-01-01&dateTo=2026-06-01 and verify date filtering

features.description: Alerts list endpoint supports sorting by severity, date, regulator, domain, and status in ascending and descending order
steps: Seed alerts with varied data
Send GET to /api/alerts?sortBy=severity&sortOrder=desc and verify high appears first
Send GET to /api/alerts?sortBy=date&sortOrder=asc and verify oldest first
Verify default sort is by date descending

features.description: Alert detail endpoint returns a single alert with full regulatory item, policy chunk (with policy document), and audit history
steps: Create an alert with associated regulatory item, policy chunk, and audit entries
Send GET to /api/alerts/:id
Verify response includes full regulatoryItem with sourceUrl, title, fullText
Verify response includes policyChunk with content and parent policyDocument
Verify response includes auditEntries array

features.description: Alert action endpoint accepts an alert (sets status to 'accepted') and creates an audit entry with before/after state
steps: Create an alert with status 'open'
Send POST to /api/alerts/:id/action with { action: 'accept' }
Verify alert status is now 'accepted'
Verify an AuditEntry was created with actor='reviewer', action='accepted'
Verify beforeState contains 'open' and afterState contains 'accepted'

features.description: Alert action endpoint dismisses an alert with a required reason code and creates an audit entry
steps: Create an alert with status 'open'
Send POST to /api/alerts/:id/action with { action: 'dismiss', reason: 'false_positive' }
Verify alert status is now 'dismissed' and dismissReason is 'false_positive'
Verify an AuditEntry was created with the reason in the note field
Send POST without reason and verify it returns a validation error

features.description: Alert action endpoint escalates an alert with an optional note and creates an audit entry
steps: Create an alert with status 'open'
Send POST to /api/alerts/:id/action with { action: 'escalate', note: 'Needs legal review' }
Verify alert status is now 'escalated' and escalationNote is populated
Verify an AuditEntry was created with action='escalated' and the note

features.description: Alert action endpoint snoozes an alert with a required snoozeUntil date and creates an audit entry
steps: Create an alert with status 'open'
Send POST to /api/alerts/:id/action with { action: 'snooze', snoozeUntil: '2026-06-01' }
Verify alert status is now 'snoozed' and snoozeUntil is set
Verify an AuditEntry was created with action='snoozed'
Send POST without snoozeUntil and verify it returns a validation error

features.description: Ingestion trigger endpoint starts an ingestion run asynchronously and returns the run ID
steps: Send POST to /api/ingestion/trigger
Verify response contains { runId: <string>, status: 'started' }
Verify an IngestionRun record was created with trigger='manual' and status='running'
Wait briefly and verify the run eventually completes or fails (not stuck)

features.description: Ingestion trigger endpoint accepts an optional URL to ingest a specific regulatory publication
steps: Send POST to /api/ingestion/trigger with { url: '<a real SEC publication URL>' }
Verify the run processes only the specified URL
Verify the resulting regulatory item has the correct sourceUrl

features.description: Ingestion log endpoint returns paginated list of all ingestion runs with metrics
steps: Create several ingestion runs with varied statuses and metrics
Send GET to /api/ingestion/log
Verify response contains runs array with id, trigger, status, startedAt, completedAt, itemsProcessed, itemsFlagged, itemsSuppressed
Verify pagination works with page and pageSize params
Verify runs are ordered by startedAt descending

features.description: Dashboard page displays three severity summary cards showing counts of high, medium, and low severity open alerts
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000
Verify three cards are visible labeled High, Medium, Low
Verify each card shows the correct count matching the database
Verify high card uses red styling, medium uses amber, low uses blue

features.description: Dashboard page displays a 'Trigger Ingestion' button that starts an ingestion run when clicked
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000
Verify a 'Trigger Ingestion' button is visible
Click the button
Verify a loading/in-progress indicator appears
Verify a success notification or status update appears when the run completes

features.description: Dashboard page displays alerts-by-regulator breakdown showing counts for SEC, FINRA, CFPB, and OCC
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000
Verify a chart or card group shows alert counts broken down by regulator
Verify SEC, FINRA, CFPB, and OCC are all represented
Verify counts match the database

features.description: Dashboard page displays alerts-by-policy-domain breakdown
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000
Verify a chart or card group shows alert counts broken down by policy domain
Verify domain names are human-readable
Verify counts match the database

features.description: Dashboard page displays recent ingestion activity showing the last 5 runs with timestamps and item counts
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000
Verify a recent activity section shows up to 5 ingestion runs
Verify each run shows timestamp, trigger type, status, and items processed/flagged counts
Verify runs are ordered most recent first

features.description: Alert list page displays a sortable table with columns: Severity, Classification, Regulator, Policy Domain, Date Detected, Status
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/alerts
Verify a table is displayed with all required columns
Verify alerts from the database are shown in the table
Click a column header and verify the table re-sorts

features.description: Alert list page has a filter bar that filters alerts by regulator
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/alerts
Locate the filter controls
Select regulator filter for 'SEC'
Verify only SEC alerts are shown in the table
Clear the filter and verify all alerts return

features.description: Alert list page has a filter bar that filters alerts by severity
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/alerts
Select severity filter for 'high'
Verify only high-severity alerts are shown
Clear the filter and verify all alerts return

features.description: Alert list page has a filter bar that filters alerts by status
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/alerts
Select status filter for 'open'
Verify only open alerts are shown
Clear the filter and verify all alerts return

features.description: Alert list page has a filter bar that filters alerts by policy domain
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/alerts
Select domain filter for a specific domain
Verify only alerts from that domain are shown
Clear the filter and verify all alerts return

features.description: Alert list page has a date range filter that filters alerts by detection date
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/alerts
Set a date range in the filter
Verify only alerts within that date range are shown
Clear the filter and verify all alerts return

features.description: Alert list page has pagination controls that navigate between pages of results
steps: Seed database with 30+ alerts
Navigate to http://localhost:3000/alerts
Verify pagination controls are visible at the bottom
Verify first page shows 25 alerts (default page size)
Click next page and verify different alerts are shown
Verify page numbers and total count are displayed

features.description: Clicking an alert row in the list navigates to the alert detail page
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/alerts
Click on an alert row
Verify the browser navigates to /alerts/[id]
Verify the alert detail page loads with the correct alert data

features.description: Alert detail page displays regulatory source text and policy passage text in a side-by-side two-panel layout
steps: Navigate to an alert detail page
Verify left panel shows regulatory source: title, date, regulator, document type, and quoted text
Verify right panel shows policy passage: document title, section heading, and quoted text
Verify both quoted texts are visually highlighted

features.description: Alert detail page shows drift classification badge, confidence score bar, severity badge, and plain-language explanation
steps: Navigate to an alert detail page
Verify classification badge is visible with correct color coding
Verify confidence score is displayed as a visual bar or percentage
Verify severity badge is visible with correct color coding
Verify plain-language explanation text is displayed below the panels

features.description: Alert detail page displays the full citation chain with regulatory source URL and policy document/section reference
steps: Navigate to an alert detail page
Verify the regulatory source URL is displayed as a clickable link
Verify the policy reference shows the document title and section heading
Verify both citations are clearly labeled

features.description: Alert detail page has Accept button that updates the alert status to 'accepted' and refreshes the view
steps: Navigate to an alert detail page with status 'open'
Click the Accept button
Verify the alert status updates to 'accepted'
Verify the status badge changes to reflect the new status
Verify a new audit entry appears in the audit history

features.description: Alert detail page has Dismiss button that opens a reason code selector and requires a reason before dismissing
steps: Navigate to an alert detail page with status 'open'
Click the Dismiss button
Verify a reason code selector appears with options: false_positive, already_addressed, not_applicable, duplicate, accepted_risk, other
Select 'false_positive' and confirm
Verify alert status updates to 'dismissed'
Verify the dismiss reason is recorded

features.description: Alert detail page has Escalate button that accepts an optional note and updates status to 'escalated'
steps: Navigate to an alert detail page with status 'open'
Click the Escalate button
Verify a text input for a note appears
Enter 'Needs legal review' and confirm
Verify alert status updates to 'escalated'
Verify the escalation note is recorded

features.description: Alert detail page has Snooze button that requires a snooze-until date and updates status to 'snoozed'
steps: Navigate to an alert detail page with status 'open'
Click the Snooze button
Verify a date picker appears
Select a future date and confirm
Verify alert status updates to 'snoozed'
Verify the snoozeUntil date is recorded

features.description: Alert detail page shows audit history timeline displaying all actions taken on the alert in reverse chronological order
steps: Navigate to an alert that has had multiple actions taken on it
Verify the audit history section is visible at the bottom of the page
Verify entries show timestamp, actor, action, and any notes
Verify entries are ordered newest first
Verify the initial 'created' entry from the system is present

features.description: Ingestion log page displays a timestamped list of all ingestion runs with items processed, flagged, and suppressed counts
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/ingestion
Verify a table or list of ingestion runs is displayed
Verify each run shows: timestamp, trigger type (scheduled/manual), status, items processed, items flagged, items suppressed
Verify runs are ordered most recent first

features.description: Ingestion log page shows error details for failed runs
steps: Trigger an ingestion run that produces errors
Navigate to http://localhost:3000/ingestion
Locate the failed run
Verify the run shows a 'failed' status badge
Verify error details are visible or expandable

features.description: Database seeded with pre-built demo alerts covering all five classification types (aligned, drifted, contradicted, ambiguous, no_material_impact) from real regulatory publications matched against the dummy corpus
steps: Run the seed script
Query alerts and verify at least one alert exists for each classification type: drifted, contradicted, ambiguous
Verify alerts span multiple regulators (SEC, FINRA, CFPB, OCC)
Verify alerts span multiple policy domains
Verify each alert has valid citations (regulatoryQuote, policyQuote, sourceUrl, policyReference)

features.description: Database seeded with sample ingestion runs so the dashboard and ingestion log have data on first load
steps: Run the seed script
Query IngestionRun table and verify at least 3 runs exist
Verify runs have varied statuses (completed, failed)
Verify runs have realistic item counts

features.description: Database seeded with sample audit entries showing reviewer actions on alerts so the audit trail is populated for demo
steps: Run the seed script
Query AuditEntry table and verify entries exist for multiple alerts
Verify entries include both system (created) and reviewer (accepted, dismissed, escalated) actions
Verify entries have before/after state and notes where applicable

features.description: Loading states are displayed for all async operations (dashboard stats, alert list, alert detail, ingestion log)
steps: Navigate to each page and observe the loading state before data appears
Verify a spinner or skeleton UI is shown during data fetch
Verify the loading state transitions to the data view once loaded

features.description: Empty states are displayed when no data matches the current filters or when tables have no data
steps: Navigate to the alerts page and apply filters that match zero alerts
Verify an empty state message is shown (e.g., 'No alerts match your filters')
Navigate to ingestion log with no runs in the database
Verify an empty state message is shown

features.description: API errors display a toast notification with the error message instead of crashing the UI
steps: Simulate an API error (e.g., temporarily break an endpoint)
Navigate to a page that calls that endpoint
Verify a toast notification appears with an error message
Verify the page does not crash or show a blank screen
